spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: coarse tier 0–4 readout. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive the demo (buttons, selects/inputs, live script) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is well covered; edge cases / failure modes from the spec (see open questions) are not exercised on this concept page.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Add the Compute Pressure escalation path so the quality drops under thermal load
openQuestions.detail: The CPU Performance API is designed to pair with Compute Pressure. A tier-4 device that goes into 'critical' pressure should fall back to tier-2 behaviour mid-call. Showing this loop closes the canonical use case rather than freezing the choice at session start.

summary: Canonical WICG use case: video-call tile scaling + AI-inference router flipping at tier 3+. Main gap: Add the Compute Pressure escalation path so the quality drops under thermal load.
